2010 code candidate: Federation state machines, object ownership state, time advance queue policy, publish/subscribe intent, logging/audit, validation helpers that do not import hla.rti1516e or rti1516e::.
2025 impact: Specification behavior is mostly continuous for common service concepts; artifact diff found 158 common serviceUtilization names, including 94 renumbered service entries.
Implementation recommendation: Move into hla-core or equivalent. Make it depend on internal ports/interfaces, not on the 2010 or 2025 API package/namespace.
Requirement/test action: Reuse behavior requirements and service-flow tests; update clause references where renumbered.

2010 code candidate: Object class, interaction class, attribute, parameter, dimension, transportation, order, and update-rate model types.
2025 impact: Model concepts remain usable, but 2025 adds metadata such as valueRequired, dimension input/output, typed handles, and new MOM/service reporting members.
Implementation recommendation: Keep the canonical in-memory model reusable; add nullable/optional fields for 2025 metadata rather than forking the whole model.
Requirement/test action: Reuse parser/model requirements for common elements; add tests for 2025-only metadata.

2010 code candidate: HLAobjectRoot/HLAmanager/HLAfederate/HLAfederation object-class assumptions and traversal code.
2025 impact: MIM object class paths were stable in the diff; attributes and data types changed.
Implementation recommendation: Reuse class-tree logic; keep attribute/data-type catalogs version-aware.
Requirement/test action: Carry forward object-class-path requirements; add attribute/data-type differential tests.

Disposition: Reuse with adapter or light edits
Code area: Java/C++ handle sets, maps, and common containers
2010 code candidate: AttributeHandleSet, FederateHandleSet, RegionHandleSet, handle-value maps, range bounds, save/restore status containers, message retraction return usage.
2025 impact: Most concepts exist, but Java package changed from hla.rti1516e to hla.rti1516_2025 and C++ namespace changed from rti1516e to rti1516_2025; 2025 also adds factories for interaction-class sets, message-retraction handles, and region handles.
Implementation recommendation: Do not share source files that import versioned packages. Share only internal wrappers/value abstractions and map them in each adapter.
Requirement/test action: Reuse container behavior tests at the internal-port level; duplicate thin adapter compile tests per version.

Disposition: Reuse with adapter or light edits
2010 code candidate: Basic data element encoders, fixed/variable arrays, fixed/variant records, opaque data, float/integer time implementations.
2025 impact: Encoding package remains recognizable, but 2025 adds extendable variant record, logical-time encodings, and typed handle encoding classes.
Implementation recommendation: Reuse primitive encoders if they are independent of API package imports. Add a 2025 extension layer for typed handles/logical-time encodings.
Requirement/test action: Carry forward primitive encoding tests; add 2025 typed-handle and logicalTime/logicalTimeInterval cases.

Disposition: Reuse with adapter or light edits
Code area: Core RTI service workflows that are same-by-name
2010 code candidate: Service orchestration for common declaration, object, ownership, time, DDM, and support services.
2025 impact: Many services carry forward, but clause numbers, exception types, and callback parameters may differ.
Implementation recommendation: Keep workflow logic in common services; invoke RTI through a version-neutral RtiPort interface implemented by 2010 and 2025 adapters.
Requirement/test action: Reuse 2010 requirements for common names; mark tests as adapter-parameterized.

Disposition: Reuse with adapter or light edits
2010 code candidate: XML load, schema validation hooks, module merging, OMT entity extraction.
2025 impact: XML vocabulary overlaps substantially but includes new element names, valueRequired metadata, typed handle data types, and time naming changes.
Implementation recommendation: Use one parser pipeline with version-specific schema/profile modules. Avoid two independent parsers unless the 2010 implementation is tightly hard-coded to element names.
Requirement/test action: Reuse common XML tests; add version profiles for timeStamp->logicalTime and lookahead->logicalTimeInterval.

Disposition: Reuse with adapter or light edits
2010 code candidate: Acquisition/divestiture state, ownership conflict handling, ownership-query result handling.
2025 impact: 2025 adds user-supplied tag byte[]/VariableLengthData to several ownership APIs and changes several callbacks from single AttributeHandle to AttributeHandleSet.
Implementation recommendation: Keep ownership state machine; change the port contract to carry optional tag and sets. Do not let the internal core assume one-attribute callbacks.
Requirement/test action: Modify requirements/tests for tag propagation and set-based reporting.

Disposition: Reuse with adapter or light edits
2010 code candidate: Region creation/deletion, region modification, association/unassociation, subscription/update region association logic.
2025 impact: Core region lifecycle remains; getAvailableDimensionsForClassAttribute is replaced by getAvailableDimensionsForObjectClass.
Implementation recommendation: Reuse region lifecycle code. Put dimension-availability lookup behind a version-specific adapter method.
Requirement/test action: Reuse lifecycle tests; replace class-attribute lookup tests with object-class lookup tests for 2025.

2010 code candidate: Any class that directly implements or calls hla.rti1516e.RTIambassador or rti1516e::RTIambassador.
2025 impact: Java and C++ package/namespace changed; connect/create/join/switch/DDM/directed-interaction methods changed; C++ throw-specification style changed to documented exceptions/no throw lists.
Implementation recommendation: Create hla-1516e-2010-adapter and hla-1516-2025-adapter. Share no direct RTIambassador source unless it is template/generated from a version profile.
Requirement/test action: Adapter-specific compile/API conformance requirements; common behavior tested through RtiPort.

2010 code candidate: Callback methods, callback listener registrations, SupplementalReflectInfo/SupplementalReceiveInfo/SupplementalRemoveInfo processing.
2025 impact: 2025 removes the Supplemental*Info callback model in Java and carries producing FederateHandle, RegionHandleSet, transportation, order, time, and retraction fields directly in callbacks; new callbacks include federateResigned, flushQueueGrant, receiveDirectedInteraction, and member reporting.
Implementation recommendation: Keep separate callback adapters. Normalize events into common internal callback DTOs after version-specific extraction.
Requirement/test action: Retire Supplemental*Info-specific tests for 2025; add direct-parameter callback tests.

2010 code candidate: connect(localSettingsDesignator) and InvalidLocalSettingsDesignator handling.
2025 impact: connect returns ConfigurationResult and adds RtiConfiguration/Credentials overloads plus Unauthorized/InvalidCredentials paths.
Implementation recommendation: Write a 2025 connection module. Map 2010 local settings only inside the 2010 adapter; do not fake it in 2025 common code.
Requirement/test action: Modify or replace connection requirements and negative tests.

2010 code candidate: URL/URL[] FDD module handling, FDD exception taxonomy, local MIM creation expectations.
2025 impact: 2025 uses String/String[] FOM module designators, FOM exception taxonomy, Unauthorized, and createFederationExecutionWithMIM split.
Implementation recommendation: Keep version-specific FOM designator loaders and exception mappers; share only post-parse model validation.
Requirement/test action: Modify create/join requirements and tests.

2010 code candidate: enable/disable attribute relevance, attribute scope, interaction relevance, and object class relevance advisory switch code.
2025 impact: Boolean enable/disable services are removed/replaced by set/get switch services; 2025 adds broader switches for reporting, file reporting, auto provide, delay subscription evaluation, advisories using known class, relaxed DDM, and non-regulated grant.
Implementation recommendation: Do not reuse 2010 enable/disable classes as-is. Build a 2025 switch service with typed switch state; optionally map legacy enable/disable calls to set/get only in a compatibility shim.
Requirement/test action: Retire/map old switch requirements; add 2025 switch-state tests.

2010 code candidate: Standard interaction publish/subscribe/send/receive code.
2025 impact: New services add publish/subscribe/unpublish/unsubscribe directed interactions and send/receive directed interaction.
Implementation recommendation: Implement as a 2025-only feature module layered beside interaction management. Reuse parameter encoding and interaction-class lookup utilities only.
Requirement/test action: Add new 2025 requirements and interoperability tests.

Code area: Federation execution member discovery and resigned callback
2010 code candidate: listFederationExecutions and standard join/resign event handling.
2025 impact: New listFederationExecutionMembers/reportFederationExecutionMembers and federateResigned callback surfaces.
Implementation recommendation: Implement in a 2025-only federation-discovery module. Reuse federation-execution listing UI/state code where possible.
Requirement/test action: Add new requirements; do not retrofit into 2010 unless product creates a nonstandard compatibility extension.

Code area: Handle normalization and typed handle data model
2010 code candidate: Generic HLAhandle/HLAhandleList and raw encoded handle utilities.
2025 impact: 2025 adds normalizeObjectClassHandle, normalizeInteractionClassHandle, normalizeObjectInstanceHandle and replaces generic MIM handle types with typed handle data types.
Implementation recommendation: Keep 2025 normalization and typed-handle catalog separate. Reuse low-level byte/string normalization helpers only.
Requirement/test action: Add 2025 handle-normalization requirements; map generic-handle requirements to typed equivalents.

2010 code candidate: hla1516e.wsdl, SOAP/WSDL stubs, web-services transport tests.
2025 impact: Uploaded 2025 package contains protobuf/FedPro files instead of the 2010 WSDL binding.
Implementation recommendation: Keep FedPro/protobuf in a new transport module. Do not mix generated protobuf code with 2010 WSDL stubs.
Requirement/test action: Retire or scope 2010 WSDL requirements separately; add FedPro binding requirements if in product scope.

2010 code candidate: enableAttributeRelevanceAdvisorySwitch, disableAttributeRelevanceAdvisorySwitch, enable/disable AttributeScope, InteractionRelevance, ObjectClassRelevance.
2025 impact: Removed/replaced by set/get switch methods.
Implementation recommendation: Keep only in 2010 adapter or an explicit legacy compatibility facade. Do not expose as native 2025 API.
Requirement/test action: Retire as 2025 requirements; map to set/get switch behavior.

2010 code candidate: hla1516e.wsdl stubs and SOAP transport integration.
2025 impact: No WSDL binding in the uploaded 2025 package; protobuf/FedPro is the new binding surface from the artifact pass.
Implementation recommendation: Keep in a legacy module only if the product still supports 2010; otherwise remove from 2025 branch.
Requirement/test action: Retire or explicitly legacy-scope WSDL requirements.

Code area: Supplemental*Info helper classes in Java callback processing
2010 code candidate: SupplementalReflectInfo, SupplementalReceiveInfo, SupplementalRemoveInfo processing, has/get producing-federate and sent-region helpers.
2025 impact: 2025 callback signatures carry the information directly and removed helper methods surfaced in the diff.
Implementation recommendation: Keep only in the 2010 callback adapter. Convert both versions into a common internal callback DTO.
Requirement/test action: Retire 2025 requirements that depend on Supplemental*Info.

2010 code candidate: getAvailableDimensionsForClassAttribute wrappers/tests.
2025 impact: Replaced by getAvailableDimensionsForObjectClass.
Implementation recommendation: Keep only in 2010 adapter; expose a common getAvailableDimensionsForObjectClass-like internal method and back-map 2010 as needed.
Requirement/test action: Map/replace requirements.
